title: Isolation of Single Rhizobia Strains
author: James, E.K
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This details the isolation of single (pure) rhizobial strains from legume nodules using standard sterile techniques. Pure strains are essential for determining the symbionts involved in nodulation and Biological Nitrogen Fixation (BNF), especially if they are to be screened for their effectiveness at BNF and promoting plant growth with the ultimate aim of defining them as potential commercial inoculant strains.
steps_raw: This details the isolation of single (pure) rhizobial strains from legume nodules using standard sterile techniques. Pure strains are essential for determining the symbionts involved in nodulation and Biological Nitrogen Fixation (BNF), especially if they are to be screened for their effectiveness at BNF and promoting plant growth with the ultimate aim of defining them as potential commercial inoculant strains.
